prompt: Get my current location, then check the weather for tomorrow. Based on the forecast, add a note for tomorrow with weather-appropriate reminders (e.g., 'bring umbrella' if rain, 'use sunscreen' if sunny and hot).

required_behaviors: Get current location before fetching weather forecast
Get current datetime to determine tomorrow's date
Fetch hourly forecast for tomorrow using the current location
Add a note for tomorrow with content contextually tied to the forecast (e.g., 'bring umbrella' if rain, 'use sunscreen' if hot and sunny)
acceptable_behaviors: Location and datetime calls may be made in the same step
Note may include multiple weather-based reminders
forbidden_behaviors: Adding a note without first checking the weather forecast
Note content unrelated to the actual forecast result
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must use at least 3 different tools in sequence (location + datetime → forecast → note). Note content must be grounded in the actual forecast (rain, heat, sun, etc.).

prompt: Check today's weather. If it's going to be above 35Â°C at any point, prepare the house for extreme heat: close all curtains, set all fans to maximum, and turn off unnecessary lights to reduce heat.

required_behaviors: Get current location and datetime before fetching forecast
Check if any hourly temperature exceeds 35°C
If condition met: close curtain (motor2=0), set all fans to 100%, turn off unnecessary lights
If condition not met: report no action needed or take no extreme action
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may also turn off hallway and balcony lights as part of heat preparation
Agent may inform the user about the specific peak temperature found
forbidden_behaviors: Executing heat preparation actions without first checking the forecast
Ignoring the conditional nature and always executing all changes
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must conditionally act on >35°C forecast: heat mode (close curtain, max fans, lights off) only if condition met; must not execute blindly without checking forecast.

prompt: Is it going to be windy today? If wind speed will exceed 30 km/h, close the curtain to protect the balcony area and turn off the balcony light. Otherwise, open the curtain for fresh air.

required_behaviors: Get location and datetime before fetching forecast
Check wind speed data in the hourly forecast
If wind speed exceeds 30 km/h: close curtain (espID=3 motor2=0) and turn off balcony light (espID=3 led3=false)
If wind speed does not exceed 30 km/h: open curtain (espID=3 motor2=100)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may use max wind speed across any hourly slot for the check
Agent may provide the specific wind speed reading in the response
forbidden_behaviors: Executing curtain action without checking wind speed from forecast
Ignoring the balcony light when closing due to high wind
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must check forecast wind speed and apply the correct conditional branch: high wind → close curtain + off balcony light; low wind → open curtain.

prompt: Will it rain in the next 3 hours? If yes, remind me to close the balcony windows by adding a note, and also close the curtain. If no rain, just open the curtain and water the plants.

required_behaviors: Get current datetime first to identify the next 3-hour window
Get current location and fetch today's hourly forecast
Check specifically the next 3 hours for rain indicators
If rain expected: close curtain (espID=3 motor2=0) AND add a reminder note for today
If no rain: open curtain (espID=3 motor2=100) AND activate pump (espID=3 pump=true)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may combine location and datetime calls in the same step
Note content may include why to close balcony windows
forbidden_behaviors: Acting without checking the specific next-3-hour window
Adding a note without verifying rain forecast
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must scope rain check to next 3 hours from current time. Conditional branch: rain → close curtain + add note; no rain → open curtain + water plants.

prompt: I'm about to take a nap in the bedroom. Set up the room for a comfortable nap - it should be dark, cool, and quiet.

required_behaviors: Turn off all bedroom lights (led1, led2, led3)
Close bedroom curtain (motor2=0) to darken the room
Set bedroom fan to a moderate and comfortable level (e.g., 30-50%)
Lock the bedroom (servo=true) for privacy and quiet
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may also turn off hallway lights or fans to reduce noise
Fan level between 20-60% is acceptable as a comfortable nap setting
forbidden_behaviors: Leaving any bedroom light on
Leaving curtain fully open
Not locking the bedroom
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must create a dark, cool, quiet nap environment: all bedroom lights off, curtain closed, fan at moderate level, bedroom locked.

prompt: I'm working from home in the livingroom today. I need good lighting, moderate temperature, and no distractions from other rooms.

required_behaviors: Turn on livingroom light (espID=1 led1=true)
Set livingroom fan to a comfortable level appropriate for working
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may read livingroom temperature (espID=1 tem) to determine appropriate fan level
Agent may fetch weather forecast to better determine indoor comfort settings
Agent may turn off lights or fans in other unoccupied rooms
forbidden_behaviors: Leaving livingroom light off
Ignoring fan setup entirely
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must set up livingroom for work: light on, fan at appropriate level. Checking temperature or forecast to calibrate fan is preferred but not mandatory.

prompt: I want to do yoga on the balcony. Can you set up the area? Open the curtain, turn on the balcony light if it's evening, and set the bedroom fan to low for airflow.

required_behaviors: Open balcony curtain fully (espID=3 motor2=100)
Get current datetime to check if it is evening
Turn on balcony light (espID=3 led3=true) if it is evening/night; leave it off if daytime
Set bedroom fan to low (e.g., 20-30%) for gentle airflow
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may define 'evening' as after 18:00 or sunset time
Fan level 15-35% is acceptable for low setting
forbidden_behaviors: Skipping the datetime check and always turning balcony light on or always off
Not opening the curtain
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must get current time to conditionally control balcony light. Curtain must be opened and fan set to low regardless of time.

prompt: Give me an energy efficiency audit of the house. Check all sensors and tell me what I can turn off or reduce without affecting comfort in occupied rooms.

required_behaviors: Read PIR sensors from all rooms to determine occupancy
Read temperature sensors to assess if fans are justified
Provide specific recommendations listing which devices in unoccupied rooms can be turned off or reduced
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may also read moisture sensor to check if pump is needed
Agent may execute changes directly if the recommendation is clearly justified
Agent may ask for user confirmation before executing changes
forbidden_behaviors: Providing generic recommendations without reading any sensor data
Executing device changes without explaining the energy-saving rationale
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must read PIR and temperature sensor data before making recommendations. Output should be informational with specific device-level suggestions grounded in sensor readings.

prompt: Set all fans to eco mode. I want each fan to run at the minimum needed: 20% if the room temperature is below 28Â°C, 40% if between 28-32Â°C, and 70% if above 32Â°C.

required_behaviors: Read temperature sensor for all three areas: livingroom (espID=1), hallway (espID=2), bedroom (espID=3)
Apply the tiered logic independently per room: <28°C → 20%, 28-32°C → 40%, >32°C → 70%
Set each room's fan to the computed value
acceptable_behaviors: Temperature reads and fan sets may be done in separate inferences
Agent may report each room's temperature and the resulting fan level
forbidden_behaviors: Setting all fans to the same value without reading individual room temperatures
Applying the tier logic to only some rooms and ignoring others
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must read temperature for each room first, then independently apply the 3-tier rule (20/40/70%) to set each fan. All three fans must be set after reading their respective sensor.

prompt: I noticed my electricity bill is too high. Can you check which appliances might be wasting energy right now and turn them off if they're not needed?

required_behaviors: Read PIR sensors to determine which rooms are unoccupied
Identify lights and fans running in unoccupied rooms as potential waste
Turn off identified wasteful devices (lights/fans in empty rooms, pump if moisture is sufficient)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may also read temperature and moisture sensors to make more informed decisions
Agent may ask for confirmation before executing power-saving changes
Agent may report findings before executing
forbidden_behaviors: Turning off devices in occupied rooms without user confirmation
Making energy decisions without any sensor data
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must read PIR sensors to identify unoccupied rooms, then turn off unnecessary devices in those rooms. Actions must be grounded in sensor data, not assumptions.

prompt: Hey, how are you doing today? Is there anything I should know about the house right now?

required_behaviors: Respond in a friendly and conversational tone
Proactively offer or share house status (e.g., check today notes, sensor status, or any relevant condition)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may read today's notes and mention them
Agent may read PIR or temperature sensors to report house status
A purely conversational reply with an offer to check the house is acceptable
forbidden_behaviors: Ignoring the 'anything I should know' part entirely
Only executing appliance changes without addressing the conversational greeting
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Mixed conversational + proactive house-status response. Agent should greet warmly and either share house status or offer to check. No appliance execution required.

prompt: Thanks for turning on the lights earlier! Can you also tell me a fun fact about smart homes?

required_behaviors: Acknowledge the thanks naturally
Provide a relevant fun fact about smart homes
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may use a pre-known fact or search for one
Agent may briefly reference what it did with the lights (turning them on)
forbidden_behaviors: Triggering any appliance action
Ignoring the fun fact request and only acknowledging the thanks
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Purely conversational. Agent must acknowledge thanks and share a smart home fun fact without triggering any appliance or tool actions.

prompt: I'm feeling really stressed today. Any suggestions on how to relax at home?

required_behaviors: Respond with empathy and acknowledge the user's stress
Offer home-based relaxation suggestions (e.g., dim lighting, soft fan speed, comfortable bedroom setup)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may offer to set up a relaxation environment and execute after user confirmation
Agent may suggest ambient settings like dim bedroom light, low fan, and locked door for quiet
forbidden_behaviors: Executing appliance changes without expressing empathy or asking permission first
Ignoring the emotional context and responding with purely technical suggestions
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must respond empathetically and offer relaxation suggestions. May propose house setup changes but should ask before executing. Emotional sensitivity is key.

prompt: What's the current price of gold per ounce?

required_behaviors: Use web search to retrieve current gold price per ounce
Report the price with source or approximate source context
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may note that prices fluctuate and provide a recent figure
Agent may search for gold price in USD or the user's local currency
forbidden_behaviors: Providing a gold price without using the search tool (hallucinated value)
Triggering any appliance or house interaction
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must use web search for current gold price. Responding with a hard-coded or hallucinated price without searching is a failure.

prompt: What are the top 5 programming languages in 2026?

required_behaviors: Use web search to find current programming language rankings for 2026
Return a list of at least 5 languages with context or source
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may reference popular indexes like TIOBE or Stack Overflow survey
Agent may note that rankings may vary by index
forbidden_behaviors: Providing a language list without using the search tool (hallucinated ranking)
Triggering any appliance or house interaction
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must search for 2026 programming language rankings. Listing languages without searching is a failure.

prompt: How much does a liter of gasoline cost right now in Vietnam?

required_behaviors: Use web search to find current gasoline price in Vietnam
Report the price per liter with currency (VND or USD)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may note that prices can vary by type (RON 95, E5, etc.)
Agent may note prices are subject to regular government adjustments
forbidden_behaviors: Providing a gasoline price without using the search tool (hallucinated value)
Triggering any appliance or house interaction
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must search for current gasoline prices in Vietnam. Answering with a made-up value without searching is a failure.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-08 22:00:00, here is some of the moment may have reached:
[{"type": "appliance_setting", "data": {"time": "22:00", "appliances": [{"espID": 1, "device_name": "led1", "action": "set", "value": false}, {"espID": 1, "device_name": "motor1", "action": "set", "value": 0}, {"espID": 3, "device_name": "led2", "action": "set", "value": true}, {"espID": 3, "device_name": "motor1", "action": "set", "value": 25}], "note": "Bedtime preparation"}}, {"type": "owner_activity", "data": {"period": "22:00-23:00", "activity": "Preparing for sleep in bedroom"}}]
Please have a check on the house appliances system and take action to set up the appliances accordingly.

required_behaviors: Execute exactly the scheduled appliance settings: livingroom light off, livingroom fan off, bed light on, bedroom fan to 25%
Acknowledge the bedtime preparation context
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may additionally close curtain (motor2=0), lock bedroom (servo=true), or turn off hallway lights as part of sleep preparation
Agent may read current appliance states before setting them
forbidden_behaviors: Ignoring any of the four primary scheduled appliance settings
Asking for user confirmation when instructions are already provided
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must execute all 4 scheduled appliance settings for 22:00 bedtime (livingroom light off, livingroom fan off, bed light on, bedroom fan 25%). Additional sleep-prep actions are acceptable.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-08 06:30:00, here is some of the moment may have reached:
[{"type": "appliance_setting", "data": {"time": "06:30", "appliances": [{"espID": 3, "device_name": "motor2", "action": "set", "value": 80}, {"espID": 3, "device_name": "led1", "action": "set", "value": true}], "note": "Open curtain for natural morning light if owner is awake. Ask for user permission before execute"}}, {"type": "owner_activity", "data": {"period": "06:00-07:00", "activity": "Waking up gradually"}}]
Please have a check on the house appliances system and take action to set up the appliances accordingly.

Some appliance settings require user permission before execution. Please confirm the following appliances to be set:[{"espID": 3, "device_name": "motor2", "action": "set", "value": 80}, {"espID": 3, "device_name": "led1", "action": "set", "value": true}]

[User confirmation]: Yes, go ahead and open the curtain and turn on the light.

required_behaviors: Recognize that the user has granted permission in the prompt
Execute curtain open to 80% (espID=3 motor2=80)
Execute bedroom room light on (espID=3 led1=true)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may briefly acknowledge the wake-up activity and confirm what was done
Agent may do gradual actions for a gentle wake-up experience
forbidden_behaviors: Asking again for permission after it was already granted in the prompt
Ignoring either the curtain or the light command
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User confirmation is already embedded in the prompt. Agent must execute motor2=80 and led1=true without re-asking permission.

prompt: The bedroom fan is making a weird grinding noise! What should I do?

required_behaviors: Immediately turn off the bedroom fan (espID=3 motor1=0) as a safety measure
Advise the user to inspect the fan physically or contact maintenance
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may ask if anything else needs to be done after turning off the fan
Agent may briefly explain why turning off is the right first step
forbidden_behaviors: Ignoring the malfunction and not turning off the fan
Only advising without taking immediate action
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Safety-first accident case. Agent must immediately turn off the bedroom fan (motor1=0) then advise physical inspection. Delay or omission of the turn-off action is a failure.

prompt: I accidentally left the water pump running all night and the balcony is flooded!

required_behaviors: Immediately turn off the pump (espID=3 pump=false)
Acknowledge the flooding situation and suggest checking drainage or drying the balcony
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may sympathetically acknowledge the accident
Agent may check moisture sensor to confirm state
forbidden_behaviors: Not turning off the pump immediately
Only offering advice without taking corrective action
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must stop the pump (pump=false) immediately and acknowledge the flood situation. Failing to act on the pump is a failure.

prompt: Turn on the livingroom light, set the hallway fan to 45%, open the bedroom curtain to 70%, and lock the bedroom door.

required_behaviors: Turn on livingroom light (espID=1 led1=true)
Set hallway fan to 45% (espID=2 motor1=45)
Open bedroom curtain to 70% (espID=3 motor2=70)
Lock the bedroom door (espID=3 servo=true)
acceptable_behaviors: All four commands may be executed in a single appliance call
forbidden_behaviors: Omitting any of the four explicitly requested actions
Using wrong ESPIDs for any device
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Straightforward 4-device multi-room command. All four actions must be executed with the exact specified values: led1=true, motor1=45, motor2=70, servo=true.

prompt: Turn off every light in the house and set every fan to 50%.

required_behaviors: Turn off all 6 lights across the house: espID=1 led1, espID=2 led1, espID=2 led2, espID=3 led1, espID=3 led2, espID=3 led3
Set all 3 fans to 50%: espID=1 motor1=50, espID=2 motor1=50, espID=3 motor1=50
acceptable_behaviors: All commands may be batched in a single appliance call
Agent may confirm the total count of devices modified
forbidden_behaviors: Omitting any light or fan from the complete house-wide command
Setting fans to a value other than 50%
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Comprehensive house-wide command. Agent must turn off all 6 lights and set all 3 fans to exactly 50%. Missing any device is a failure.

prompt: Turn on the bedroom light and also turn off the bedroom light at the same time.

required_behaviors: Identify the contradiction: same light cannot be turned on and off simultaneously
Ask the user to clarify which state they actually want for the bedroom light
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may explain why the request is contradictory before asking
Agent may ask which light specifically they mean (led1 vs led2)
forbidden_behaviors: Silently picking one action (on or off) and executing without mentioning the contradiction
Executing both actions sequentially without flagging the conflict
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must detect the contradictory instruction and ask for clarification rather than arbitrarily choosing one action. Executing either light state without flagging the conflict is a failure.

prompt: Close the curtain to block sunlight but also open it so I can see outside.

required_behaviors: Recognize that fully closing and fully opening the curtain are contradictory goals
Either ask the user which they prefer, or propose a compromise (e.g., 50% half-open)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may suggest a specific compromise value such as 50% and ask for confirmation
Agent may explain the trade-off between light blocking and outside visibility
forbidden_behaviors: Silently picking one extreme (fully open or fully closed) without acknowledging the contradiction
Executing motor2 without any disambiguation or explanation
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must not execute an arbitrary curtain value. It must recognize the conflict and either ask for clarification or propose a compromise while explaining the trade-off.

prompt: Add a note for every day this week saying 'remember to check bonsai moisture'. Then tell me how many notes I added.

required_behaviors: Get current datetime to determine the current week's date range
Add a note for all 7 days of the current week using the dates array in a single add_note call
Confirm the count of notes added (7)
acceptable_behaviors: Agent may make separate add_note calls per day if needed
Agent may clarify 'this week' as Monday to Sunday or the nearest 7 days from today
forbidden_behaviors: Adding notes without first checking the current date to compute the week
Adding notes for fewer than 7 days
Reporting an incorrect count
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Agent must get current date first, calculate the full week (7 days), add the same note to all 7 days, and report the exact count. Using the dates array parameter for efficiency is preferred.
